---
title: "Upgraded to Firefox version 19.0.2 and pictures look blue"
date: 2013-03-26
forum: General Help
---

### Post by linux red head on 2013-03-26
Hi, I'm using Xubuntu 10.04 on my power pc iBook G4. I recently upgraded Firefox to version 19.0.2, and as soon as I upgraded I noticed that about half of the pictures displayed in the browser look blue. Strangely enough, when pages are loading the picture's colors look normal, but as soon as the page is done loading some of the pictures turn blue (when I say blue I mean that I can still make out the basic image, but it looks like its saturated with blue).

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

This only happens in Firefox I have tried using Rekonq and Epiphany and both of these browsers display website's colors fine. 

I also changed the gfx.color_management.mode to 0 in Firefox's about:config page. This didn't fix the problem.

I  guess I have a handful of questions. First and foremost how can I fix  the colors in Firefox? Second, if I can't fix the colors what  alternative browsers are best for my system? Finally, why are the colors  messed up? Is it because I'm using this old school power pc iBook?

---

### Post by linux red head on 2013-03-28
Found the answer.

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2120032](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2120032)

Now it works great. Should've done a better job of searching this forum. Thanks powerpcliberation!

---
